title: DevKit Review-Issue — Findings Input
description: Schema for the findings JSON document the slash-command markdown (LLM-side) writes and the `devkit review-issue post` Python harness validates before composing/posting the consolidated comment. Loaded by the harness via `importlib.resources.files('aidevkit.schemas')`; no `$id` is set because the schema is private to this package and not published at any URL.

schema_version.description: Findings input schema version. Bumped on breaking changes.

policy_overrides.description: Optional per-run policy override the slash command can supply (e.g., when the operator passes flags that map to gate-policy adjustments).

findings.description: Ordered list of findings emitted by the analyzer. Order MUST be: anti-patterns first, then completeness, then derived-negative pairs, then ambiguity, then nits (per research.md R11).

id.description: Stable per-run finding ID (F01, F02, ...). Assigned in emission order. Resets on each run (per research.md R11).

location.description: Where in the issue the finding applies (e.g., 'Acceptance Criteria, item 3', 'Body §Summary').

summary.description: Single-line description, fits in the table cell.

recommendation.description: Suggested action or rewrite. Markdown allowed.

evidence.description: Optional quoted snippet from the issue body that triggered the finding. Markdown allowed.

pair.description: Required when category == derived-negative-pair. Names the positive and negative ACs in the same issue and the operator dispositions per FR-010.

positive_ref.description: Reference to the positive AC inside the issue (e.g., 'AC item 1: "..."').

negative_ref.description: Reference to the negative AC inside the issue (e.g., 'AC item 2: "..."').

entailment_explanation.description: Optional one-line explanation of why the positive entails the negative.

dispositions.description: Exactly three dispositions corresponding to FR-010: (a) drop the negative AC, (b) keep as non-goal documentation only, (c) escalate to risk-control invariant.
